content:
Member-only story

4 min read
Just now

--

In the previous article, we explored modern Java features such as virtual threads, records, Spring Boot 3.x, cloud-native development, and practical DSA applications.
Now, let us enter one of the most underrated parts of backend engineering: logging.
Many developers treat logging as a simple System.out.println(). In production systems handling millions of requests, logs become the black box recorder of your application. Good logs save hours. Bad logs create confusion.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

This article covers:

• Modern logging architecture.

• Log levels and their usage.

• Correlation IDs.

• Structured JSON logging.

• MDC implementation.

• Distributed tracing.

• ELK stack.

• Logging design patterns.

• How senior engineers read logs.
Why Logging Matters
Imagine your payment service crashes at 2 AM.
Users report:

• Money debited.

• Order not created.

• Notification missing.
